claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sigma(n) >= n + 1) and ((sigma(n) == n + 1) == is_prime(n))

claim: If n is a product of two distinct primes, then sigma(n) >= n + 3.
predicate: (len([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) != 2) or (sigma(n) >= n + 3)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is not prime, then sigma(n) >= n + 2.

claim: If n is a semiprime (product of two primes, not necessarily distinct), then sigma(n) <= n + sqrt(n) + 1.
